burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality but significant hospitalization burden
burden.mortalityRate: Low; higher with purulent or tuberculous pericarditis

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: At least two of: pleuritic chest pain, pericardial rub, typical ECG changes, or pericardial effusion

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; diagnosis in emergency settings
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with NSAIDs/colchicine; recurrence in a subset

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: ECG and echocardiography available in referral centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: NSAIDs plus colchicine; treat underlying cause; steroids for select cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Delayed diagnosis in remote areas and TB-related cases
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Colchicine availability and standardized cardiology protocols
